---
name: usafb-local-ssl
description: Local SSL setup for usafb-public-ui development. Use when starting the local SSL proxy, troubleshooting HTTPS for local.usafootball.com, or configuring mkcert certificates for this project.
---

# USA Football Local SSL Setup

## SSL Proxy for Local Development

To run the app with HTTPS at `https://local.usafootball.com:3001`, use the SSL proxy with the existing certs.

**Cert locations** (generated by mkcert, stored in home directory):
- Key: `~/certs/local.usafootball.com+3-key.pem`
- Cert: `~/certs/local.usafootball.com+3.pem`

**Start the proxy** (proxies port 3001 → 3000):
```bash
npx local-ssl-proxy --key ~/certs/local.usafootball.com+3-key.pem --cert ~/certs/local.usafootball.com+3.pem --source 3001 --target 3000
```

## Prerequisites

1. **Hosts file**: `/etc/hosts` must include:
   ```
   127.0.0.1 local.usafootball.com
   ```

2. **mkcert** (if certs don't exist yet):
   ```bash
   brew install mkcert
   mkcert -install
   mkcert -key-file ~/certs/local.usafootball.com+3-key.pem -cert-file ~/certs/local.usafootball.com+3.pem local.usafootball.com localhost 127.0.0.1 ::1
   ```

## Dev Workflow

1. Start the app on port 3000 (e.g. `yarn dev` or `npm run dev`)
2. Start the SSL proxy (command above)
3. Open `https://local.usafootball.com:3001`
